concept: Forgive / Release Grievance / Let Go of Resentment
description: To release resentment toward one who has caused harm, freeing both self and other from the weight of grievance. 'Tholira' names the inner liberation that forgiveness brings—not condoning harm but choosing to set down the burden of bitterness, making space for healing.
sound_symbolism: The word releases as it speaks. 'Tho' /θo/ begins with gentle breath, the decision to let go. Then 'li' lifts with liquid lightness, burden releasing. Finally 'ra' rolls outward with warm extension, forgiveness given.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mi ru tholira' (I forgive you). Can specify what is forgiven: 'mi ru lo themo tholira' (I forgive you for something). Related to leiro (release) but specifically addresses grievance and harm.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Forgiveness releases attachment to resentment—we suffer less when we set down the poison of grievance, regardless of whether others deserve our forgiveness.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Forgiveness enables relationship to continue or conclude peacefully—it transforms the meaning of past harm without erasing it.
